Tests the behavior of the Math.sin function with negative zero input.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the JavaScript `Math.sin` function when given the input `-0`. The test verifies that dividing `1` by the result of `Math.sin(-0)` yields `-Infinity`. This assertion ensures that the mathematical operation conforms to expected behavior in JavaScript, particularly in handling edge cases involving negative zero. The code is narrow in functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical property and its correct implementation in the JavaScript environment.
